title: GitHub API の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article contains a significant inaccuracy regarding the Authorization header format, stating that 'token' should be used instead of 'Bearer', whereas GitHub documentation indicates both are generally acceptable, with 'Bearer' required for JWTs.
The claim about PATs having a maximum validity period of 100 years is incorrect; fine-grained PATs have a maximum custom lifetime of 1 year, and classic PATs can be set to have no expiration.
Minor discrepancies exist in the 'documentation_url' provided in the example curl outputs when compared to actual GitHub API responses.
The article is generally up-to-date with current GitHub API practices and features like fine-grained PATs, and most claims are well-supported by official documentation.
absolute wording may overstate technical certainty

sources.title: Authenticating to the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: GitHub APIで 401 Unauthorizedエラーが発生するのは、リクエストに対する認証が失敗した場合です。このエラーは、認証情報が完全に欠落している、形式が正しくない、または無効な状態を示しています。

sources.title: [BUG] GitHub Analyzer API Returning 401 Unauthorized Error · Issue #840 · S3DFX-CYBER/GSoC-Org-Finder-
sources.claim: GitHub APIで 401 Unauthorizedエラーが発生するのは、リクエストに対する認証が失敗した場合です。このエラーは、認証情報が完全に欠落している、形式が正しくない、または無効な状態を示しています。

unsupported_claims: PATには最大100年の有効期限を設定できますが、明示的に有効期限を設定している場合は有効期限の管理が必要です。(The maximum validity period of 100 years for PATs is not supported by official documentation; fine-grained PATs have a maximum of 1 year, and classic PATs can be non-expiring.)

citation_mismatches.actual: curl https://api.github.com/user returns documentation_url: https://docs.github.com/rest/users/users#get-the-authenticated-user (The path segment 'users/' is missing in the claimed URL.)

citation_mismatches.claimed: curl -H "Authorization: token invalidtoken" https://api.github.com/user returns documentation_url: https://docs.github.com/rest
citation_mismatches.actual: curl -H "Authorization: token invalidtoken" https://api.github.com/user returns documentation_url: https://docs.github.com/rest/overview/resources-in-the-rest-api#authentication (The claimed URL is too broad and does not point to the specific authentication section.)

citation_mismatches.claimed: GitHub APIは特定の形式でAuthorizationヘッダーを受け取ります。`Bearer`ではなく`token`キーワードを使用する必要があります。(GitHub API accepts Authorization headers in a specific format. The 'token' keyword should be used instead of 'Bearer'.)
citation_mismatches.actual: GitHub documentation states: 'In most cases, you can use Authorization: Bearer or Authorization: token to pass a token. However, if you are passing a JSON web token (JWT), you must use Authorization: Bearer.' This indicates both 'token' and 'Bearer' are accepted, and 'Bearer' is sometimes required, directly contradicting the article's exclusive recommendation of 'token'.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Correct the statement regarding the Authorization header format to accurately reflect that both 'token' and 'Bearer' are accepted by the GitHub REST API, and 'Bearer' is required for JWTs.
Optional improvement: Revise the maximum PAT lifetime claim to state that fine-grained PATs have a maximum custom expiration of 1 year, and classic PATs can be set to never expire.
Optional improvement: Update the 'documentation_url' in the example curl outputs to precisely match the URLs returned by the GitHub API for those specific error messages.
Optional improvement: Consider adding a direct citation or rephrasing the claim about header spacing errors if a primary source cannot be found, although it is logically sound.
Optional improvement: Soften absolute claims unless they are directly sourced.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
